lessons.title: First Steps with Python
lessons.description: Write a program to display 'Hello, World!'

lessons.title: Variables and Calculations
lessons.description:
Write a program that assigns your age to a variable, calculates your age in 10 years, and displays it.
For example, if you're 14 now, you'll be 24 in 10 years.

lessons.title: Data Types and Conversion
lessons.description:
Write a program that converts two numbers given as strings to integers, calculates their sum, and displays it.
Finally, display the result in the format "The sum is ○○".

lessons.title: User Input
lessons.description:
Use the input() function to ask the user for their name and return a greeting.
Display the greeting in the format "Hello, ○○!"

lessons.title: Debugging Basics
lessons.description:
The following code has 3 bugs. Read the error messages and fix all the bugs.
After fixing them, verify that it works correctly.
Expected behavior:

  Display "Taro-kun will be 16 years old next year"
  Display "Height is 160cm, and if it grows 10cm, it will be 170cm"

chapters.title: Conditional Statements
chapters.description: Learn to control program flow with conditions

lessons.title: Basics of Conditional Statements
lessons.description:
Create a program that asks the user to enter a number, then determines if it's positive, negative, or zero.
Examples:

  Input: 5 → Output: Positive number
  Input: -3 → Output: Negative number
  Input: 0 → Output: Zero

chapters.title: Data Structures
chapters.description: Learn to work with collections of data

chapters.title: Practical Projects
chapters.description: Apply your knowledge to build real programs
